text: {"analysis_summary": "Reviewed the visible RTL under the provided scope for permission/security-control logic. The strongest issues are in the FC subsystem interrupt/event-unit integration: security/privilege-related signals exist but are tied off, unconnected, or not used in register write paths. The APB interrupt controller permits APB writes to interrupt mask, pending, and acknowledge state solely based on APB transfer strobes, with no visible privilege/secure-mode authorization. The FC subsystem also advertises supervisor mode as permanently asserted. These are permission-related weaknesses if any untrusted or less-privileged software/master can reach these APB-visible controls or if downstream logic relies on supervisor_mode_o for access control.", "findings": [{"finding_id": "FC-PERM-001", "status": "confirmed_finding", "summary": "APB interrupt-control registers are writable without visible permission checks, and secure-mode context is ignored/tied off.", "vulnerability_category": "Missing authorization / insecure privilege gating for control registers", "affected_locations": [{"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": 47, "line_end": 47, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "signal_or_register": "core_secure_mode_i"}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": 90, "line_end": 90, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "signal_or_register": "s_is_apb_write"}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": 131, "line_end": 138, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "signal_or_register": "r_mask / s_mask_next"}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": 159, "line_end": 166, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "signal_or_register": "r_int / s_int_next"}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": 177, "line_end": 184, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "signal_or_register": "r_ack / s_ack_next"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 283, "line_end": 283, "module": "fc_subsystem", "signal_or_register": "core_secure_mode_i"}], "evidence": [{"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": 47, "line_end": 47, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "object": "core_secure_mode_i", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "The interrupt controller has an explicit secure-mode input, suggesting writes or interrupt security could be mode-dependent.", "supports_claim": "A security/permission context signal exists on the module interface."}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": 90, "line_end": 90, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "object": "s_is_apb_write", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "APB write detection is based only on psel, penable, and pwrite.", "supports_claim": "The write-enable condition contains no secure-mode, privilege, address-protection, or requester check."}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": 131, "line_end": 138, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "object": "s_mask_next / r_mask", "evidence_type": "source_lines", "description": "Mask register update logic accepts direct APB writes, set, and clear operations using apb_slave.pwdata whenever s_is_apb_write is true.", "supports_claim": "Interrupt enable/mask state is writable without a visible permission check."}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": 159, "line_end": 166, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "object": "s_int_next / r_int", "evidence_type": "source_lines", "description": "Interrupt pending register update logic accepts APB writes, set, and clear operations using apb_slave.pwdata whenever s_is_apb_write is true.", "supports_claim": "Software-visible APB writes can inject or clear interrupt pending bits without visible authorization."}, {"file": "ips/apb_interrupt_cntrl/apb_interrupt_cntrl.sv", "line_start": 177, "line_end": 184, "module": "apb_interrupt_cntrl", "object": "s_ack_next / r_ack", "evidence_type": "source_lines", "description": "Acknowledge register update logic accepts APB writes, set, and clear operations using apb_slave.pwdata whenever s_is_apb_write is true.", "supports_claim": "Interrupt acknowledge state is writable without a visible permission check."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 283, "line_end": 283, "module": "fc_subsystem", "object": "core_secure_mode_i", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "The FC subsystem ties the interrupt controller secure-mode input to constant 0.", "supports_claim": "Integration does not propagate any real core security/privilege state into the interrupt controller."}], "reasoning_summary": "The APB interrupt controller contains security-related interface signals, but the write paths for mask, interrupt-pending, and acknowledge registers are gated only by APB transaction strobes. The visible source shows no use of core_secure_mode_i in those write decisions, and the FC subsystem ties that input to 1'b0. As a result, the controller itself provides no permission enforcement for APB writes to interrupt-control state.", "security_impact": "A less-privileged or compromised APB master/software context that can reach this APB slave could mask interrupts, fabricate pending interrupts, clear pending events, or manipulate acknowledge state. This can cause denial of service, interrupt spoofing, privilege-boundary bypass through interrupt control, or suppression of security-critical events.", "confidence": "high", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "The reviewed scope does not include the complete APB interconnect or memory map. An upstream firewall could mitigate access to this peripheral, but no such enforcement is visible in the provided files.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Add explicit authorization gating for APB writes to interrupt mask, pending, acknowledge, and FIFO-related controls, using a real privilege/secure-mode indication or APB protection metadata if available.", "Do not tie core_secure_mode_i to a constant unless the design intentionally has no security domains; connect it to actual core/security state and verify intended policy.", "Check the APB interconnect outside this scope for address/firewall enforcement. If no upstream enforcement exists, restrict the event-unit register region to trusted/supervisor code only."]}, {"finding_id": "FC-PERM-002", "status": "potential_warning", "summary": "FC subsystem permanently reports supervisor mode, eliminating privilege distinction for downstream permission checks.", "vulnerability_category": "Privilege signal hardwired / missing privilege separation", "affected_locations": [{"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 44, "line_end": 44, "module": "fc_subsystem", "signal_or_register": "supervisor_mode_o"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 273, "line_end": 273, "module": "fc_subsystem", "signal_or_register": "supervisor_mode_o"}, {"file": "ips/zero-riscy/zeroriscy_cs_registers.sv", "line_start": 163, "line_end": 170, "module": "zeroriscy_cs_registers", "signal_or_register": "mstatus_q / mpp"}, {"file": "ips/zero-riscy/zeroriscy_cs_registers.sv", "line_start": 196, "line_end": 201, "module": "zeroriscy_cs_registers", "signal_or_register": "mstatus_n.mpp"}], "evidence": [{"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 44, "line_end": 44, "module": "fc_subsystem", "object": "supervisor_mode_o", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "The FC subsystem exposes supervisor_mode_o as an output signal.", "supports_claim": "There is an integration-level signal that can represent privilege/supervisor status to other logic."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/fc/fc_subsystem.sv", "line_start": 273, "line_end": 273, "module": "fc_subsystem", "object": "assign supervisor_mode_o = 1'b1", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "The FC subsystem permanently drives supervisor_mode_o high.", "supports_claim": "Any downstream permission logic using this signal would always see the core as supervisor-privileged."}, {"file": "ips/zero-riscy/zeroriscy_cs_registers.sv", "line_start": 163, "line_end": 170, "module": "zeroriscy_cs_registers", "object": "mstatus CSR", "evidence_type": "source_lines", "description": "CSR read logic comments that mstatus is 'always M-mode' and returns mstatus_q.mpp as part of mstatus.", "supports_claim": "The core CSR implementation appears to model only machine-mode privilege state rather than tracking user/supervisor transitions."}, {"file": "ips/zero-riscy/zeroriscy_cs_registers.sv", "line_start": 196, "line_end": 201, "module": "zeroriscy_cs_registers", "object": "mstatus_n.mpp", "evidence_type": "source_lines", "description": "Writes to mstatus force mpp to PRIV_LVL_M.", "supports_claim": "The visible CSR implementation does not allow mstatus.MPP to represent a lower privilege level."}], "reasoning_summary": "The top-level FC subsystem always asserts supervisor_mode_o, and the Zero-Riscy CSR implementation is annotated and implemented as always machine mode for mstatus.MPP. If supervisor_mode_o is consumed by interconnects, memories, or peripherals as an access-control attribute, this hardwire grants supervisor-level permission unconditionally and prevents hardware from distinguishing less-privileged execution contexts.", "security_impact": "Downstream access-control logic relying on supervisor_mode_o could be bypassed because every transaction from the FC core appears supervisor-privileged. This can allow access to memory-mapped registers or memory regions intended only for privileged code.", "confidence": "medium", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "The provided scope does not show downstream consumers of supervisor_mode_o, so the exploitability depends on SoC-level integration outside the visible files. The Zero-Riscy implementation may intentionally be machine-mode-only, which would make this a design limitation rather than an implementation bug.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Determine all consumers of supervisor_mode_o in the full SoC integration and verify whether it controls access to protected memory/peripherals.", "If privilege separation is required, derive supervisor_mode_o from actual core privilege state rather than tying it to 1'b1.", "If the core intentionally supports only machine mode, document that there is no intra-core privilege isolation and ensure protected resources are isolated by other hardware mechanisms."]}], "no_finding_reason": "", "global_uncertainty": "The analysis was limited to visible source under the provided scope. Some security properties may be enforced by modules not included here, such as APB interconnect firewalls, memory protection units, or top-level access-control wrappers. No simulation, formal verification, or external documentation was used."}
